name: CREUTZFELDT-JACOB DISEASE
definition: rare subacutely progressive neurological disorder due to transmissible infectious disease

demographics.Histo: classified as spongiform encephalopathy "mad cow disease"
demographics.Location: cerebral cortex, basal ganglia (caudate nucleus), thalamus

distribution: cerebral cortex, basal ganglia (caudate nucleus), thalamus
discriminating_features: hyperintense T2 lesions in basal ganglia (head of caudate nucleus + putamen), occipital
high-SI lesions on DWI (← vacuolization of neutrophil leading to gliosis + astrocytosis and
restricted diffusion is more sensitive than T2-FLAIR, especially for cortical lesions ←

MR: hyperintense T2 lesions in basal ganglia (head of caudate nucleus + putamen), occipital
high-SI lesions on DWI (← vacuolization of neutrophil leading to gliosis + astrocytosis and
restricted diffusion is more sensitive than T2-FLAIR, especially for cortical lesions ←
"pulvinar" sign / "hockey stick" sign = T2 prolongation + restricted diffusion in medial

clinical.Prognosis: usually fatal within 2 year of onset of symptoms (mean, 8.8 months)
